title: Backend Load Remediation - Startup DDL Isolation
scope: DAO initialization behavior, startup latency, deployment safety
problem: DAO constructors execute CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS during runtime initialization, which couples application startup to schema-management work.
risk: During autoscaling, cold starts, or concurrent deployments, app instances can add avoidable startup latency and potentially contend on database metadata or locks.

evidence.note: MapInfoDAO constructor runs index creation checks on initialization.
SangkwonDAO constructor does the same for search-related indexes.

goals: Separate schema-management concerns from request-serving startup.
Keep required indexes documented and reproducible.
Avoid repeating DDL checks on every app boot.

implementation_plan.action: Remove DDL side effects from DAO constructors.
details: DAO instantiation should not execute CREATE INDEX statements.
Replace constructor-time checks with plain operational code paths.

implementation_plan.action: Move index management to an explicit migration or ops path.
details: If the repo already has a migration convention, add the required indexes there.
If not, create a documented one-time SQL artifact or admin script rather than silent runtime DDL.
Document which indexes are required for searchDong and similar prefix queries.
If runtime validation is still desired, make it non-DDL and clearly fail or warn without mutating schema.

acceptance_criteria: Application startup no longer performs CREATE INDEX work from DAO constructors.
Required indexes are captured in an explicit migration or operational artifact.
Search-related functionality remains intact after the refactor.
verification: Run affected DAO and router tests.
Verify application startup succeeds without schema mutation side effects.
If an explicit SQL artifact is added, review it for correctness against current query predicates.
execution_notes_for_claude_code: This item may touch developer workflow rather than only runtime code. Keep the change small but explicit, and do not silently drop the knowledge of required indexes.
